primary_indicator.name: Task-Relevant Visual Engagement
primary_indicator.type: direct behavioral attention indicator and operator-support proxy
primary_indicator.measures: Percentage of valid gaze time directed to the instruction paper or assembly.

interpretation.task_relevant_visual_engagement: The operator directed most valid gaze toward the instruction paper or assembly. This indicates visible task engagement, not task success or skill.
interpretation.instruction_reliance: Relevant gaze was almost evenly divided between instructions and assembly. This describes instruction reliance; it is not inherently good or bad without comparison recordings.
interpretation.entropy: AOI allocation was broadly distributed. Entropy describes spread and predictability, but requires a baseline before it can indicate efficient or inefficient search.

research_sources.title: Exploring the Effect of Visual Cues on Eye Gaze During AR-Guided Picking and Assembly Tasks
research_sources.relevance: Uses gaze distribution, gaze duration, and gaze-path measures in picking and assembly.

research_sources.title: Quantifying the predictability of visual scanpaths using active information storage
research_sources.relevance: Describes gaze-transition entropy as a measure of scanpath predictability.

research_sources.title: Detecting Worker Attention Lapses in Human-Robot Interaction
research_sources.relevance: Shows that industrial attention is complex and should not be inferred from a simplistic single-modality performance score.

research_sources.title: Exploring Eye Tracking to Detect Cognitive Load in Complex Virtual Reality Training
research_sources.relevance: Cognitive-load inference requires additional ocular features and workload ground truth, not AOI dwell alone.
